lead_level_notes.notes: All EMDR/misinformation, lie-language, cleanliness/moral, and mindbody self-centrality local files are child artifacts under the curated GitHub source family, not root source families.

cluster_decisions.reason: Cluster aliases point to one reusable GitHub source family plus several dataset-level downloads. The checked repository is an explicit curated collection of publicly available original-replication datasets and cleaned data/code artifacts; the local EMDR, lie-language, cleanliness-moral, and mindbody self-centrality hits are child artifacts, not separate root source families.

lead_level_notes.notes: OSF e5nvr paper-, experiment-, and effect-level final-analysis tables, dictionaries, and per-study R scripts are child artifacts under RP:CB.

cluster_decisions.reason: The cluster is a coherent OSF final-analysis package for RP:CB, with paper-, experiment-, and effect-level data plus dictionaries and study scripts. RP:CB is an existing reusable corpus/source family, so these are child artifacts of that parent rather than new source families.

cluster_decisions.reason: The cluster is the local raw-corpus side of the same RP:CB source family. It contains reusable final-analysis tables and dictionaries for paper, experiment, and effect levels; the included/raw RPCB parent should own these duplicates and child artifacts.

cluster_decisions.reason: This is an individual Registered Replication Report/meta-analysis cluster, not a corpus/database source family. It does contain apparent result-table artifacts for Srull/Wyer plus analysis code, so it belongs in result-table review under the RRR project rather than corpus-source intake.

lead_level_notes.notes: OSF download metadata, import script, README, file list, and manifest describe one replication package; route away from corpus intake.

cluster_decisions.next_action: Send to the individual replication-paper worklist; inspect README and OSF file inventory only if paper-level/result-table extraction is needed, but do not create a source-family root from these filenames alone.
cluster_decisions.reason: The compact evidence shows a single OSF-harvested replication package with an import script, README, download metadata, and OSF file inventory. It does not yet show an explicit reusable multi-row source-family table or corpus/database page, so route away from corpus intake.

lead_level_notes.notes: Keep the Zenodo/GitLab repository as the preferred root candidate; article and preprint hits are context.

cluster_decisions.next_action: Keep as a source-family candidate keyed to the Zenodo/GitLab repository; inspect the zip/file inventory for reusable study-pair or effect-size tables, then map any RPP/RPEE-derived child tables to their originating project parents where appropriate.
cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: Heyard and Held heterogeneity_in_replication_projects code/data repository
cluster_decisions.reason: The cluster is not just an isolated article hit: checked sources show an article/preprint plus a Zenodo/GitLab code-and-data repository for analyses of original-replication study-pairs in large-scale replication projects. The repository is a reusable source object, but its internal files still need inventory before any table-level integration.

lead_level_notes.notes: Internal derived catalog/report is provenance context only and must not enter corpus/database intake.

cluster_decisions.next_action: Keep for mapping/provenance context only; do not add it to corpus/database intake and do not edit root tables from this review.
cluster_decisions.reason: This cluster is an internal derived catalog/report, not an external dataset, database, repository, registry, or source-family landing page. It can support provenance decisions but should not be ingested as a source-family candidate.

lead_level_notes.notes: SCORE orig_outcomes, repli_outcomes, repro_prereg-check, OSF metadata, and the existing preregistration-indicated subset are child artifacts/views under SCORE.

cluster_decisions.next_action: Inventory orig_outcomes, repli_outcomes, repro_prereg-check, and OSF manifest/meta files under the SCORE parent; preserve the existing score_cos_preregistration_indicated_original_papers subset row and avoid duplicate root creation.
cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: Systematizing Confidence in Open Research and Evidence (SCORE) data/code/materials
cluster_decisions.reason: SCORE is an existing reusable source family with openly available data/code/materials. The cluster contains child data tables, an OSF manifest, and an included preregistration-indicated subset; these should be owned by the broader SCORE parent, with the subset kept as an included derived corpus view.

notes: Normalized cluster-level routing from the pasted Codex/GPT review. Detailed per-node notes from the paste are collapsed here to stable cluster actions so downstream DAG steps can consume the decisions.
